rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MARY SWAYNE MARTIN
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #2, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 6/18/24. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) The Notice of Hearing did not mention the Exhibits A through J, etc., that were filed SEPARATELY from the petition for some reason. (References in the petition to "attached" exhibits are false.) (2) There was no vehicle or any other tangible personal property inventoried, so expenses related to them are not permissible. (3) Costs to be reimbursed to the administrator and the attorney must show the date incurred and the original payee. (4) Attorney costs must be fully itemized. For example, lump sums for e-filing fees or publication fees or referee costs (commission) must show each charge separately, so that the Court can determine if they are accurate. (5) The register of actions does not reflect a $435 filing fee for "Status Report" (see subparagraph b of paragraph 31). (6) Why is the administrator requesting that administration costs (check order costs and DMV costs for a non-existent vehicle) be deducted from the 31 Lobos property only? (7) Although commendable, under what authority is the administrator requesting that the residuary gift not be abated? [Probate Code Section 21402.] Have the other beneficiaries of the specific gifts consented? (8) Brenda Pearson and Gregory Martin survived the decedent. If the residuary gift is to be made, it must be made to their successors-in-interest. [LRSF 14.35 G.] Please provide courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 6/7/24. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (The executor was appointed on 11/18/20; the order was not filed until 11/20/20. Prob. C. Sect. 9202(b) also requires notice if a BENEFICIARY has previously been confined in a prison or facility under the jurisdiction of the Department of Corrections, etc. In order for this Court to track closed matters, orders to terminate and distribute an estate must begin with the following language: "Order for Final Distribution." [LRSF 14.35 I.6.b.] When papers are not filed electronically, each paper presented for filing must contain two prepunched normal-sized holes, centered 2-1/2 inches apart and 5/8 inch from the top of the paper. [CRC 2.115.] This includes proposed orders. Except for exhibits, each paper filed with the court must bear a footer in the bottom margin of each page, placed below the page number and divided from the rest of the document page by a printed line. [CRC 2.110(a).] There is no need to recount the continuances of prior hearings or to mention the graduated filing fee, which was deemed unconstitutional long before this estate was opened.)

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE CARTE REVOCABLE TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Redress Of Breach Of Trust, Removal Of Trustee, For Recovery Of Damages, For Attorney'S Fees And Costs
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 06/25/2024 for an incomplete response to the issues posted in the tentative ruling on April 2, 2024. Issues necessitating a continuance: 1) At least 30 days before the time set for hearing on the petition, the petitioner shall cause notice of the hearing to be delivered pursuant to Section 1215 to all trustees and all beneficiaries (Probate Code Section 17203). The notice of hearing filed April 17, 2024, indicates service of the notice and the petition on April 16, 2024, just 28 days prior to the hearing scheduled for May 14, 2024.. 2) All notices must include language about remote appearances (LRSF 14.1.C.6). 3) A petition involving a trust must state the names and last known addresses of all vested or contingent beneficiaries (CRC 7.902). 4) No proposed order has been received (LRSF 14.7.A). Please provide a proposed order, courtesy copies of the petition and any other papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner no later than June 11, 2024.
